title: Design a tool-call contract
description: Specify each model-invocable tool's purpose, typed parameters, side effects, and failure shape before wiring it to the model.
body: Steps: 1. Enumerate the tools the model may call and mark which have side effects. 2. For each, define typed, constrained parameters (enums, id formats, ranges) and a typed result/error. 3. Decide which tools need a permission gate. 4. Write the validator that checks returned arguments against the schema before execution. 5. Add tests for valid, malformed, and out-of-range arguments. Rollback point: before exposing a new side-effecting tool to the model. Validation: malformed arguments are rejected and a side-effecting call without a gate fails the test.
